name: Condensed milk, sweetened (food medium)

components.name: sucrose
components.source: USDA FDC 1097540 total sugars 54.4 g minus milk lactose; partition from Sweetened Condensed Milk composition (ScienceDirect Topics; Codex STAN 282)
components.evidence: Added cane/beet sucrose is ~43-45% of SCM by weight and the dominant carbon/energy source; 54.4 g total sugars - ~11 g lactose.

components.name: lactose
components.source: Sweetened Condensed Milk overview (ScienceDirect Topics): 10-11% lactose; consistent with USDA FDC 1097540 total sugars 54.4 g
components.evidence: Milk sugar concentrated ~2.5x from fluid milk (4.8% -> ~11%); second fermentable sugar after sucrose.

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Only trace free glucose from minor sugar hydrolysis during heat processing; not routinely quantified. Null, present in trace.

components.name: D-galactose
components.evidence: Trace free galactose from minor lactose hydrolysis; not quantified. Null.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Derived: UHT cow milk citrate 1.33 g/L = 0.133 g/100mL (retail-milk organic-acid study, PMC10705155) concentrated ~2.5x for SCM
components.evidence: Citrate is the dominant organic acid of non-fermented milk; concentrated with the milk solids in SCM. MISSING from current record.

components.name: lactic acid
components.source: General dairy chemistry (fresh non-fermented milk ~0.01-0.02 g/100mL)
components.evidence: SCM is NOT fermented, so lactic acid is only trace/low; included for completeness. Low confidence.

components.name: orotic acid
components.source: Orotic acid content in cow's milk (Agrarforschung Schweiz 2008; ~60-90 mg/L fluid) concentrated ~2.5x
components.evidence: Characteristic cow-milk pyrimidine constituent; minor. MISSING from current record.

components.name: total protein (casein + whey)
components.evidence: Casein-dominant milk protein; amino acids below are the protein-bound profile released on proteolysis.

components.name: L-Phenylalanine
components.evidence: Protein-bound AA profile. MISSING from current record.

components.name: L-Valine
components.evidence: Protein-bound AA profile. MISSING from current record.

components.name: L-Serine
components.evidence: Corrects current record's D-Serine (milk serine is the L-enantiomer).

components.name: Total fat (milk fat)
components.evidence: Milk fat; individual fatty acids below.

components.name: Butyric acid (C4:0)
components.evidence: Short-chain milk fatty acid. MISSING from current record.

components.name: Myristic acid (C14:0)
components.evidence: Major saturated milk FA. MISSING from current record.

components.name: Palmitic acid (C16:0)
components.evidence: Most abundant fatty acid in milk fat. MISSING from current record.

components.name: Stearic acid (C18:0)
components.evidence: Major saturated milk FA. MISSING from current record.

components.name: Palmitoleic acid (C16:1)
components.evidence: Monounsaturated milk FA. MISSING from current record.

components.name: Oleic acid (C18:1)
components.evidence: Dominant unsaturated milk FA. MISSING from current record.

components.name: Linoleic acid (C18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Essential PUFA. MISSING from current record.

components.name: alpha-Linolenic acid (C18:3 n-3)
components.evidence: Essential PUFA. MISSING from current record.

components.name: Cholesterol
components.evidence: Genuine dairy sterol (KEEP; not FooDB noise here because this is an animal food).

components.name: Iron
components.evidence: Single iron entry (current record had 3 duplicate iron species).

components.name: Molybdate (molybdenum)
components.evidence: Essential trace element (Mo cofactor); present at trace, not quantified in USDA. Kept, not noise.

components.name: Chloride
components.evidence: Major milk anion (~1000-1200 mg/L fluid milk, concentrated in SCM); USDA does not tabulate. Present, unquantified.

components.name: Sulfate
components.evidence: Minor milk anion / sulfur source; unquantified. Kept.

components.name: Ammonium
components.evidence: Minor free-N source (milk NPN includes ammonia/urea); unquantified. Kept as minor N.

components.name: beta-Carotene
components.source: USDA FDC 1097540 (provitamin A carotenoid, low in milk fat)

remove.name: CO2 CO2
remove.reason: Dissolved gas / BiGG artifact, not a growth nutrient of the food matrix

remove.name: O2 O2
remove.reason: Dissolved gas, not a food nutrient

remove.name: H+
remove.reason: Proton / pH descriptor, not a nutrient

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: Free cobalt ion is non-nutritive trace noise; biologically relevant cobalt is delivered as vitamin B12 (retained)

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: Compartment-tagged BiGG artifact and duplicate of iron; collapsed to a single Iron entry

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: Duplicate iron species; collapsed to a single Iron entry

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: Wrong stereoisomer; milk serine is L-serine (added). D-serine is a FooDB/BiGG artifact

missing_added: lactose (milk sugar, major fermentable, absent from current record)
citric acid (dominant organic acid of non-fermented milk)
Palmitic acid C16:0 (single most abundant milk FA)
keep_uncovered: orotic acid (may lack a BiGG met but is a real milk pyrimidine)
notes: Dominant carbon/energy source is ADDED SUCROSE (~43 g/100g), then milk LACTOSE (~11 g/100g); together ~54 g total sugars (USDA FDC 1097540). This is a sweetened, non-fermented, concentrated whole-milk product, so lactic acid is only trace and citric acid (concentrated from fluid milk, ~0.33 g/100g) is the main organic acid. Full amino-acid, fatty-acid, vitamin and mineral panels are USDA FDC 1097540 (mirrored via nutritionvalue.org). Basis is mass per 100 g product; concentration_mM left null because the per-100g mass basis has no defined volume (dense product ~1.29 g/mL).
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 1097540 (Milk, sweetened, condensed, canned) via nutritionvalue.org and myfooddata.com mirrors
Sweetened Condensed Milk composition overview, ScienceDirect Topics (fat 8-9%, non-fat solids 20-22%, lactose 10-11%, sucrose 43-45%, moisture 25-27%)
Retail cow-milk organic-acid / fatty-acid study, PMC10705155 (citrate ~1.33 g/L in UHT milk)
Orotic acid content in cow's milk, Agrarforschung Schweiz 2008
Fatty acids in bovine milk fat review, PMC2596709 / doi:10.3402/fnr.v52i0.1821
